title: Refreshing Canadian Chicken Burger
description: A citrusy, crunchy Canadian favorite featuring a tangy horseradish slaw, double-fried KFC‑style chicken patty, and a nostalgic tagliere presentation.
instructions: In a bowl combine ground chicken, cornstarch, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper and form into two flat patties.
Whisk lemon zest, orange juice, and prepared horseradish together to create a fresh citrusy sauce for the slaw.
Heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the patties for 4 minutes on each side until browned.
Transfer patties to a wire rack and let them rest for 2 minutes before adding the remaining 2 tbsp oil and continuing to fry for an additional 3 minutes on each side to achieve a double‑fried finish.
Meanwhile, toss sliced red onion and pickles with the citrus horseradish sauce and set aside on a tagliere for serving.
Spread a thin layer of melted butter on each bun and toast them lightly in a dry skillet for 1 minute.
Panic the toasted buns with the citrus slaw, place the double‑fried chicken patty on top, and finish with a few extra drops of citrus sauce.
Serve immediately on the tagliere and enjoy the crunch and citrus zing of this Canadian chicken burger.
